description: Phase 4.2 component decomposition: components with id / name / domain_id / responsibilities (atomic statements) / dependencies, covering every SR.

template_variables.active_constraints:
1. The Governed Stream is lossless. All execution trace content — reasoning steps, self-corrections, tool call invocations, tool results — is captured in full regardless of what subset is used for any given downstream purpose. (Authority 7, source: 55a61a95-64fd-475b-a985-7962936fb07e)
2. No governing constraint may be truncated silently. Governing constraints (Authority Level 6+) are always delivered in full via the stdin directive channel. (Authority 7, source: b680da1d-f6d6-4113-ab4d-1a166154aff7)
3. Prompt Templates use namespace prefixing ([JC:] and [P:]) and separate context scopes at all times. (Authority 7, source: a67d4502-972e-4c08-b482-19fbc402a5e9)
4. All Artifacts are owned by JanumiCode and stored in the Governed Stream database. (Authority 7, source: a1030128-7334-4d7a-8ae9-8900de3c2993)
5. The Governed Stream is single-threaded. No parallel Workflow Runs in a Workspace. (Authority 7, source: 5c77f777-a698-405b-9881-b6813ae04ec5)
6. Agents never exercise judgment. Judgment is always escalated to the human. (Authority 7, source: b3e3ec17-574d-4915-99a2-244e0e71639e)
7. Every human interaction is recorded in the Governed Stream in full detail. (Authority 7, source: d7f5ea89-7265-4416-847f-348290ea28a2)
8. Every Phase Gate requires human approval. No automated gate passage. (Authority 7, source: ba84c364-c2f2-4a63-86ff-f3a49aba6c19)
9. Every phase is mandatory and executed in order. The Orchestrator cannot skip phases. (Authority 7, source: 0450a160-f9ec-4e41-ab94-188f37bf1794)
10. 100% correctness and completeness — always. All three layers of correctness (Section 1.6) are required. (Authority 7, source: 4394e1a8-f6d1-42c0-a25c-7991e6d02a5e)
template_variables.software_domains_summary:
JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine: Link Lifecycle Engine (reqs: SR-001, SR-006, SR-009, SR-010, SR-011, SR-016)
  Terms: Slug: The six-character short identifier generated for original URLs to enable public sharing without authentication.; OriginalUrl: The target destination address stored and retrieved via the LinkMapping, encrypted at rest using AES-256.; LinkMapping: The unique, non-duplicated association record between a Slug and an OriginalUrl within the content repository.; DeletionRequest: A user-initiated request from a Sharer to remove a specific link from persistent storage and ephemeral cache within 24 hours.; DataExportJob: A system process that exports 100% of stored link data for a requesting user to satisfy GDPR Right to Access requests.; LinkExpiryEvent: A behavioral cluster triggered when a link policy reaches its expiry timestamp, initiating notification attempts for every policy-expiry event.
JC:Security-Validation-Gateway: Security Validation Gateway (reqs: SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015)
  Terms: MaliciousRequest: A request identified as malicious or invalid during validation that is rejected to prevent processing.; SecureChannel: The enforced HTTPS transport layer with HSTS headers and HTTP redirection used for all public endpoints.; RateLimitCounter: The enforcement mechanism limiting requests to 1000 per IP per rolling 60-second window, rejecting excess with HTTP 429.; ThreatIndicator: An indicator from the Threat Intelligence Feed used to block submissions and redirects within 5 seconds latency.; MaliciousSubmission: A URL content flagged as malicious before storage in the content repository to prevent persistence of harmful links.
JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations: Platform Reliability Operations (reqs: SR-003, SR-004, SR-005, SR-007, SR-012, SR-017)
  Terms: RedirectLatency: The P95 latency metric monitored via Prometheus histograms to ensure redirect endpoint latency remains within 100 ms under nominal load.; UptimeMetric: The verified uptime metric calculated over rolling 30-day windows via synthetic probes, ensuring availability ≥ 99.9% including planned maintenance.; RecoveryObjective: The Recovery Time Objective (RTO) target defined for full regional failure recovery, requiring restoration within 15 minutes.; AuditLogRecord: The structured JSON log entry emitted to stdout for ingestion, retaining audit logs for a minimum of 365 days.; BackupReplicationJob: The automated task executed daily to backup data and replicate with zero integrity errors to disaster recovery storage.; SystemTimeout: A system state condition where timeout handling is applied to prevent service crashes during operational delays.
template_variables.system_requirements_summary:
17 System Requirements:
  SR-001 [critical]: System shall perform successful mapping lookup for valid slug, returning encrypted payload metadata and target destination. (traces to: ["FR-002-001.1"])
  SR-002 [critical]: System shall validate and reject malicious or invalid requests to prevent processing. (traces to: ["US-001-C1-1"])
  SR-003 [high]: System shall monitor redirect endpoint latency P95 via Prometheus histograms under nominal load to maintain latency ≤ 100 ms within rolling 5-minute windows. (traces to: ["NFR-001","NFR-001-D-001"])
  SR-004 [high]: System shall verify monthly uptime for redirect endpoint including planned maintenance via synthetic probes to ensure verified uptime ≥ 99.9% over rolling 30-day windows. (traces to: ["NFR-002","NFR-AVAIL-002-D-001"])
  SR-005 [high]: System shall achieve Recovery Time Objective (RTO) on full regional failure ≤ 15 minutes. (traces to: ["NFR-003"])
  SR-006 [high]: System shall store original URLs encrypted at rest using AES-256 cipher suite with zero plaintext occurrence in direct reads. (traces to: ["NFR-004"])
  SR-007 [high]: System shall emit structured JSON logs to stdout for ingestion by platform, retaining audit logs ≥ 365 days and alerting on ingestion failure < 1 minute. (traces to: ["NFR-005"])
  SR-008 [high]: System shall enforce HTTPS on all public endpoints with HSTS headers and redirect HTTP requests. (traces to: ["NFR-006"])
  SR-009 [high]: System shall ensure every stored slug in the mappings table is unique with no duplicates. (traces to: ["NFR-007"])
  SR-010 [high]: System shall purge valid deletion requests from Sharers from persistent storage and ephemeral cache within 24 hours of receipt. (traces to: ["NFR-008"])
  SR-011 [high]: System shall export 100% of stored link data for a requesting user for GDPR Right to Access requests with zero data loss. (traces to: ["NFR-009"])
  SR-012 [high]: System shall execute daily automated backups and replicate with zero integrity errors to ensure 100% job success. (traces to: ["NFR-010"])
  SR-013 [high]: System shall block threat indicators with latency ≤ 5 seconds and update threat feeds ≤ 15 minutes. (traces to: ["NFR-011"])
  SR-014 [high]: System shall enforce rate limit of 1000 requests per IP per rolling 60-second window and reject excess with HTTP 429. (traces to: ["NFR-012"])
  SR-015 [high]: System shall block malicious URL submissions before storage in content repository. (traces to: ["NFR-013"])
  SR-016 [high]: System shall generate notification attempts for every policy-expiry event before the expiry timestamp with zero missed notifications. (traces to: ["NFR-014"])
  SR-017 [high]: System shall handle timeout conditions without triggering service crashes. (traces to: ["NFR-015"])

responsibilities.statement: Validates and rejects malicious or invalid requests to prevent processing.
Enforces HTTPS transport layer with HSTS headers and HTTP redirection.
Blocks threat indicators within 5 seconds latency to prevent harmful submissions.
Enforces rate limit of 1000 requests per IP per rolling 60-second window.
Blocks malicious URL submissions before storage in content repository.
Performs successful mapping lookup for valid slug, returning encrypted payload metadata and target destination.
Stores original URLs encrypted at rest using AES-256 cipher suite with zero plaintext occurrence in direct reads.
Ensures every stored slug in the mappings table is unique with no duplicates.
Purges valid deletion requests from Sharers from persistent storage and ephemeral cache.
Exports 100% of stored link data for a requesting user for GDPR Right to Access requests.
Generates notification attempts for every policy-expiry event before the expiry timestamp.
Monitors redirect endpoint latency P95 via Prometheus histograms to ensure latency remains within 100 ms.
Verifies monthly uptime for redirect endpoint via synthetic probes to ensure verified uptime ≥ 99.9%.
Emits structured JSON logs to stdout for ingestion by platform, retaining audit logs for a minimum of 365 days.
Achieves Recovery Time Objective (RTO) on full regional failure requiring restoration within 15 minutes.
Executes daily automated backups and replicates with zero integrity errors to disaster recovery storage.
Handles timeout conditions without triggering service crashes.

baseline.thinking:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** [JC:Architecture Agent] performing Component Decomposition for Sub-Phase 4.2.
    *   **Task:** Decompose the system into [JC:Components] with defined responsibilities and dependencies.
    *   **Input:** Governing Constraints, Context (Software Domains, Terms, System Requirements).
    *   **Output Format:** JSON object matching the `component_model` schema.
    *   **Constraints:** Strict adherence to governing constraints (lossless stream, no truncation, namespace prefixing, etc.), specific invariant rules (CM-001 atomicity, CM-002 existence, SR allocation completeness).
    *   **Domains:**
        *   JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine (SR-001, SR-006, SR-009, SR-010, SR-011, SR-016)
        *   JC:Security-Validation-Gateway (SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015)
        *   JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations (SR-003, SR-004, SR-005, SR-007, SR-012, SR-017)
    *   **Schema Requirements:** `components` array, each with `id`, `name`, `domain_id`, `responsibilities` (array of `{id, statement}`), `dependencies` (array of `{target_component_id, dependency_type}`).
    *   **Hard Rules:**
        *   Responsibility atomicity (no conjunctions).
        *   SR allocation completeness (trace every SR).
        *   JSON structure compliance.

2.  **Analyze System Requirements & Domains:**

    *   **JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine:**
        *   SR-001: Mapping lookup, return encrypted metadata & destination. (Core Business Logic: Storage & Retrieval)
        *   SR-006: Store original URLs encrypted at rest. (Core Business Logic: Encryption/Storage Policy)
        *   SR-009: Ensure slug uniqueness. (Core Business Logic: Data Integrity/Constraints)
        *   SR-010: Purge deletion requests within 24h. (Core Business Logic: Lifecycle Management/Deletion)
        *   SR-011: Export 100% data for GDPR. (Core Business Logic: Data Export/Privacy)
        *   SR-016: Generate notification attempts for policy-expiry. (Core Business Logic: Event Notification)

    *   **JC:Security-Validation-Gateway:**
        *   SR-002: Validate/reject malicious/invalid requests. (Core Business Logic: Input Validation)
        *   SR-008: Enforce HTTPS, HSTS, redirect. (Core Business Logic: Transport Security)
        *   SR-013: Block threat indicators. (Core Business Logic: Threat Intelligence Integration)
        *   SR-014: Rate limiting (1000 req/IP/60s). (Core Business Logic: Rate Limiting)
        *   SR-015: Block malicious URL submissions. (Core Business Logic: Content Safety)

    *   **JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations:**
        *   SR-003: Monitor redirect latency P95. (Core Business Logic: Observability/Metrics)
        *   SR-004: Verify monthly uptime via synthetic probes. (Core Business Logic: Health Checks)
        *   SR-005: Achieve RTO on regional failure. (Core Business Logic: Disaster Recovery)
        *   SR-007: Emit structured JSON logs, retain 365 days. (Core Business Logic: Logging/Auditing)
        *   SR-012: Execute daily backups/replication. (Core Business Logic: Backup/Recovery Ops)
        *   SR-017: Handle timeouts without crashes. (Core Business Logic: Fault Tolerance/Resilience)

    *   **Observation:** While the context lists domains with their associated requirements, the actual decomposition needs to break these down into specific *Components* that handle these responsibilities. A "Domain" isn't necessarily a single "Component" in this schema, unless specified. However, the prompt asks to "Decompose the system into [JC:Components]". Usually, this implies deriving specific logical components (e.g., API Gateway, Data Store Service, Audit Logger, etc.) rather than just replicating the provided Domain names unless those Domains represent single monolithic components. Given the strict SR allocation, I should create components that logically map to the *functions* defined by the requirements, potentially grouping them under the Domain IDs provided in the context.
    *   *Correction:* The prompt says "Context: Software Domains: ...". It lists domains with specific IDs (e.g., JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine). The output schema requires `domain_id`. I should use the Domain names as `domain_id` for the components that belong to them.
    *   *Constraint Check:* SR-001 (Lookup) vs SR-006 (Encryption) vs SR-009 (Uniqueness). These often belong to a "Link Service" or "Core Engine". SR-002, SR-008, SR-014 are Gateway functions. SR-007, SR-012, SR-017 are Ops functions.
    *   *Decision:* I will create granular components that represent the functional units.
        1.  **LinkLookupService** (SR-001)
        2.  **LinkPersistenceLayer** (SR-006, SR-009)
        3.  **LifecycleManager** (SR-010, SR-016)
        4.  **DataExportService** (SR-011)
        5.  **SecurityGateway** (SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015) -> Actually, Rate Limiting (SR-014) and Threat Blocking (SR-013) and Malicious Check (SR-015) and HTTPS (SR-008) are often grouped in a Gateway. SR-002 (Malicious Request) is also security.
        6.  **ObservabilityCollector** (SR-003)
        7.  **HealthMonitor** (SR-004)
        8.  **DROrchestrator** (SR-005, SR-012)
        9.  **AuditLogger** (SR-007)
        10. **ResilienceHandler** (SR-017)

    *   *Refinement:* I need to ensure SR-013 (Threat Feed latency) might need a component distinct from general security if it requires a specific feed manager.
    *   *Refinement:* SR-017 (Timeout handling) is usually a cross-cutting concern (middleware/aspect) but in component decomposition, it often maps to a `ResilienceLayer` or specific `FaultTolerance` component.
    *   *Constraint:* "Every Component Responsibility statement must be a SINGLE concern".
    *   *Constraint:* "Every System Requirement must be allocated to at least one Component".
    *   *Constraint:* "If a Responsibility is too broad for a single Executor Agent session, it is an implementability_violation".
    *   *Schema:* `components`: array. Each: `id`, `name`, `domain_id`, `responsibilities`, `dependencies`.

3.  **Drafting the Components:**

    *   **Component 1: APIGateway (JC:Security-Validation-Gateway)**
        *   SR-002 (Validation), SR-008 (HTTPS), SR-014 (Rate Limiting).
        *   SR-013 (Threat Feed) -> Could be integrated or separate. Let's keep it integrated in Security if it's about blocking incoming threats.
        *   SR-015 (Malicious Submission).
        *   Responsibilities:
            1.  Validate input requests against security policies.
            2.  Enforce secure transport protocols.
            3.  Limit request frequency per IP.
            4.  Block malicious URL content.
            5.  Integrate threat indicators for blocking.
        *   Wait, SR-013 says "update threat feeds ≤ 15 minutes". This is more of a management task. Maybe split?
        *   Let's stick to functional units.
        *   Component: `SecurityValidationUnit`.
        *   Responsibilities:
            1.  Validate and reject malicious or invalid requests.
            2.  Enforce HTTPS and HSTS headers.
            3.  Enforce rate limit counter.
            4.  Block threat indicators.
            5.  Block malicious URL submissions.
        *   *Critique:* "Enforce rate limit", "Enforce HTTPS", "Block Threats", "Block Malicious URLs", "Validate Requests" are distinct. If I put them all in one component, it's one component "SecurityValidation". Is it too broad? The prompt says "If a Responsibility is too broad...". A component can handle multiple responsibilities. But the *Responsibility statement* inside the component must be atomic.
        *   Let's create a component `SecurityAccessLayer` with responsibilities like "Validates request integrity", "Enforces transport security", "Applies rate limiting", etc.
        *   SR-013 mentions "update threat feeds". That implies a data source management. Maybe `ThreatIntelligenceService`?
        *   Let's group closely related ones.
        *   Component `SecurityValidationGateway` handles SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015.
            *   Res 1: Validates and rejects malicious or invalid requests.
            *   Res 2: Enforces HTTPS with HSTS headers.
            *   Res 3: Blocks malicious submissions before storage.
            *   Res 4: Blocks threat indicators from feed.
            *   Res 5: Enforces rate limit windows.
        *   Wait, SR-013 has a feed update task. I'll include it.
        *   Dependencies: None internal? Maybe external Threat Feed. Schema says `target_component_id`. I assume internal dependencies only.
        *   Domain ID: `JC:Security-Validation-Gateway`

    *   **Component 2: LinkCoreEngine (JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine)**
        *   SR-001 (Lookup), SR-006 (Encryption), SR-009 (Uniqueness).
        *   Responsibilities:
            1.  Performs successful mapping lookup for valid slug.
            2.  Stores original URLs encrypted at rest.
            3.  Ensures unique slug constraint.
        *   Dependencies: None? Maybe `SecurityValidationGateway` (for SR-002 overlap? No, SR-002 is Gateway. SR-001 is Lookup).
        *   Domain ID: `JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine`

    *   **Component 3: DataLifecycleManager (JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine)**
        *   SR-010 (Deletion), SR-011 (Export), SR-016 (Notification).
        *   Responsibilities:
            1.  Purges valid deletion requests from storage.
            2.  Exports 100% of stored link data for GDPR.
            3.  Generates notification attempts for policy-expiry.
        *   Domain ID: `JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine`
        *   Dependency: `LinkCoreEngine` (needs data store).

    *   **Component 4: ReliabilityOpsService (JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations)**
        *   SR-003 (Latency), SR-004 (Uptime), SR-005 (RTO), SR-007 (Logs), SR-012 (Backup), SR-017 (Timeouts).
        *   This is a mix of Observability, DR, and Logging. In microservices, these are often distinct components (Metrics, Logging, Backup). But the context groups them under `JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations`.
        *   To avoid "Responsibility Atomicity" violation, I might need to split `Platform-Reliability-Operations` into specific components *or* group them carefully within responsibilities.
        *   Splitting is safer for atomicity.
        *   However, the `domain_id` in the schema likely corresponds to the Domain provided in context.
        *   If I split `ReliabilityOpsService` into `MonitoringAgent`, `AuditLogger`, `BackupJob`, `ResilienceHandler`, they will all have `domain_id: JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations`.
        *   SR-003, SR-004 -> MonitoringComponent.
        *   SR-007 -> AuditLoggerComponent.
        *   SR-012, SR-005 -> BackupComponent.
        *   SR-017 -> ResilienceComponent.
        *   This ensures atomicity within the `responsibilities` arrays (one statement per responsibility).
        *   Wait, I need to check if the schema allows multiple components per Domain. Yes, `components` array.
        *   Let's create:
            1.  `ObservabilityCollector`: SR-003, SR-004.
            2.  `AuditLogEmitter`: SR-007.
            3.  `BackupReplicationJob`: SR-012.
            4.  `ResilienceErrorHandler`: SR-017.
            5.  `RecoveryOrchestrator`: SR-005 (partially overlap with Backup, but RTO is DR policy). Maybe merge Backup/Recovery? SR-005 is RTO (Time), SR-012 is Backup (Data). I'll merge them into `DisasterRecoveryUnit` to keep domain consistency or split them.
            *   Decision: Split for atomicity. SR-005 (RTO) vs SR-012 (Backup).
            *   Let's define components based on the SR distribution.
            *   Actually, looking at the domains list: "JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations (reqs: SR-003, SR-004, SR-005, SR-007, SR-012, SR-017)".
            *   If I create a component `ReliabilityOperations`, and put all responsibilities there, each responsibility statement must be atomic.
            *   Statement 1: "Monitors redirect endpoint latency P95...".
            *   Statement 2: "Verifies monthly uptime...".
            *   Statement 3: "Achieves Recovery Time Objective...".
            *   Statement 4: "Emits structured JSON logs...".
            *   Statement 5: "Executes daily automated backups...".
            *   Statement 6: "Handles timeout conditions...".
            *   Are these "too broad"? No, they are distinct behaviors. A single component can have multiple responsibilities if they are distinct atomic statements.
            *   However, typically "Platform Reliability" is an aspect across the system. But here I must define components.
            *   I will group them to match the Domain logic but ensure the statements inside are atomic.
            *   Wait, SR-005 (RTO) and SR-012 (Backup) are closely related (Backup is the mechanism to meet RTO). SR-003 (Latency) is monitoring. SR-004 (Uptime) is health.
            *   I will create specific components to align with architectural best practices (Separation of Concerns) even if they share the same Domain ID.
            *   Components:
                1.  `MetricsCollector` (SR-003, SR-004)
                2.  `AuditLogger` (SR-007)
                3.  `RecoveryManager` (SR-005, SR-012)
                4.  `ResilienceHandler` (SR-017)
            *   This ensures atomicity and clear boundaries.

    *   **Dependencies:**
        *   `LinkCoreEngine` depends on `SecurityValidationGateway`? Yes, requests pass through Gateway.
        *   `LinkCoreEngine` might depend on `AuditLogger` (for logs).
        *   `SecurityValidationGateway` depends on `ResilienceHandler`? (If Gateway handles timeouts).
        *   `DataLifecycleManager` depends on `LinkCoreEngine` (needs to read/write the mapping data).
        *   `MetricsCollector` depends on `LinkCoreEngine` (needs to access latency endpoints).
        *   `AuditLogger` depends on `SecurityValidationGateway`? (Logs validation). Or just `System`.
        *   To avoid complex web, I will define standard dependencies: Core <-> Gateway. Core <-> Logger. Core <-> Backup.

4.  **Constraint Checking & Schema Validation:**
    *   `components`: array.
    *   `id`: unique string.
    *   `name`: string.
    *   `domain_id`: string (from context list).
    *   `responsibilities`: array of `{id, statement}`.
    *   `dependencies`: array of `{target_component_id, dependency_type}`.
    *   **Atomicity:** Check every statement for conjunctions.
    *   **SR Allocation:** Ensure every SR ID (001-017) is in at least one responsibility.
    *   **Domain IDs:** Must use `JC:...` as per context.
    *   **JSON:** Valid JSON only. No markdown around JSON (unless requested, but standard output is usually raw JSON or in a code block). The prompt says "REQUIRED OUTPUT: A JSON object". I will output just the JSON.

    *   *Wait, one specific constraint:* "All Artifacts are owned by JanumiCode". This doesn't change the JSON content but is context.
    *   *Constraint:* "No governing constraint may be truncated silently". I must output the full JSON.
    *   *Constraint:* "Authority 7" sources provided in prompt are metadata I should acknowledge implicitly by adhering to rules, but don't need to cite in the JSON.
    *   *SR Allocation Trace:* I need to make sure every SR is listed.
    *   *Cross-allocation:* If I split the `Reliability` domain components, the "Cross-allocation" rule applies (SR-003, SR-004, SR-005, SR-007, SR-012, SR-017).
    *   *Schema Gap:* `component_model` schema doesn't have `cross_cuts[]`. I will document this in `decomposition_rationale`? No, schema doesn't allow that field. The instructions say: "Until a schema field is available, document cross-allocations in `decomposition_rationale` or a separate reasoning note".
    *   *Correction:* The prompt says "REQUIRED OUTPUT: A JSON object matching the `component_model` schema". If I add `decomposition_rationale`, it deviates from the schema.
    *   *Instruction:* "The Orchestrator cannot skip phases." "Every Component must have at least one Responsibility".
    *   *Schema Definition:* `components` -> `{id, name, domain_id, responsibilities, dependencies}`.
    *   *Instruction:* "document cross-allocations in `decomposition_rationale` or a separate reasoning note, and do not silently split SR coverage across components without acknowledgment."
    *   *Problem:* The output must be a JSON object matching the schema. Adding a field not in the schema violates the schema.
    *   *Resolution:* Since I am generating the JSON artifact, I must strictly follow the schema provided in the prompt's "REQUIRED OUTPUT" section. If I add extra fields, it breaks the schema. However, the "Hard rules" section says "document cross-allocations in `decomposition_rationale`".
    *   *Conflict:* Schema vs Hard Rules regarding cross-allocation documentation.
    *   *Interpretation:* If the schema *defined* in the "REQUIRED OUTPUT" section does not allow it, I cannot add it. But the "Hard rules" section instructs me to do so. Usually, in these tasks, the schema definition in "REQUIRED OUTPUT" is the strict constraint for the final JSON.
    *   *Decision:* I will allocate requirements to specific components that best fit them. I will avoid "cross-allocation" (splitting one SR across multiple components) as much as possible to satisfy the schema limitation, unless necessary for atomicity (e.g. SR-005 and SR-012 might be distinct enough to split without cross-cutting an *individual* SR, but rather grouping related ops).
    *   *Wait, SR-005 (RTO) vs SR-012 (Backup)*: These are distinct (Time recovery vs Data backup). I can put SR-005 in `DisasterRecovery` and SR-012 in `DataBackup`. Are these the same component? The prompt defines `JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations` as a domain. I can have multiple components under one domain.
    *   *Better approach:* Group closely related responsibilities to minimize cross-allocation ambiguity.
    *   *Plan:*
        1.  `LinkLookupService` (SR-001, SR-009, SR-006 - Storage is often linked to Lookup).
        2.  `SecurityValidationService` (SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015).
        3.  `LinkLifecycleService` (SR-010, SR-011, SR-016).
        4.  `ObservabilityService` (SR-003, SR-004).
        5.  `AuditService` (SR-007).
        6.  `ResilienceService` (SR-017, SR-005).
        7.  `BackupService` (SR-012).
        *   *Check SR-006 (Encryption):* Usually a capability of the Data Store or Security. I'll put it with Link Storage (`LinkCoreStorage`).
        *   *Check SR-013 (Threat Feed):* It involves updating feeds.
        *   *Check SR-012 (Backup) & SR-005 (RTO):* I will group them into `RecoveryBackupService` to keep reliability domain cohesive, or split. Splitting SR-012 to `BackupService` and SR-005 to `RecoveryService` under same domain is fine.
        *   *Check Cross-allocation:* If I split Backup and Recovery, I risk looking like I'm splitting SR-005 and SR-012 logic. They are distinct. SR-012 is "Execute daily automated backups". SR-005 is "Achieve RTO". Distinct concerns.
        *   *I will create multiple components under JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations to respect atomicity.*
        *   *Dependencies:* `LinkLookupService` depends on `SecurityValidationService`. `LinkLifecycleService` depends on `LinkLookupService`. `RecoveryBackupService` depends on `LinkLookupService`.

    *   *Refining Component IDs:*
        *   `LinkCoreStorage`: SR-001, SR-006, SR-009.
        *   `LinkLifeCycleEvents`: SR-010, SR-016, SR-011.
        *   `SecurityValidationNode`: SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015.
        *   `ReliabilityMonitoring`: SR-003, SR-004.
        *   `ReliabilityAudit`: SR-007.
        *   `ReliabilityRecovery`: SR-005, SR-012.
        *   `ReliabilityResilience`: SR-017.
        *   *Wait, SR-011 (Export)* is also Lifecycle.
        *   *Let's optimize:*
            *   `JC:Link-Lifecycle-Engine`:
                *   `LinkMappingRepository`: SR-001, SR-006, SR-009. (Read/Write/Encryption/Constraint)
                *   `LinkLifecycleProcessor`: SR-010, SR-011, SR-016. (Purge, Export, Notify).
            *   `JC:Security-Validation-Gateway`:
                *   `SecurityValidationNode`: SR-002, SR-008, SR-013, SR-014, SR-015.
            *   `JC:Platform-Reliability-Operations`:
                *   `ObservabilityCollector`: SR-003, SR-004.
                *   `AuditLogManager`: SR-007.
                *   `DisasterRecoveryManager`: SR-005, SR-012.
                *   `FaultToleranceHandler`: SR-017.

    *   *Constraint Check:* "Every Component Responsibility statement must be a SINGLE concern".
    *   *Statement Check:*
        *   SR-001: "System shall perform successful mapping lookup for valid slug, returning encrypted payload metadata and target destination." -> "Performs successful mapping lookup for valid slug and returns encrypted payload metadata and target destination."
